Field Lists

Field Lists

A component's field list determines how Uniface manages the fields when retrieving occurrences into the component at runtime. The field list includes not only the fields that are part of the component data structure, but also other fields that may be required, for example, because they are referenced in ProcScript or part of a key.

If there are fields in an entity that are not needed for a component to function properly, Uniface does not load them into the component at runtime. By default, Uniface does this automatically and all DBMS connectors make use of this facility. However, you can explicitly instruct Uniface to load all fields, or you can define your own field list.

Field lists can enhance performance for presentation components because Uniface does not need to maintain, convert, and format excluded fields in memory. Excluded fields are always retrieved from the database, but Uniface does not format or convert them. When the required fields are stored, Uniface adds the excluded fields and sends a complete record back to the database.

By default, the field list for presentation components is automatic, meaning that Uniface determines which fields are required. A field is included in the automatic field list if it meets one of the following conditions:

  • It is included in the component data structure.
  • It is part of a foreign, candidate or primary key.
  • It is referred to in a ProcScript statement. Only fields that are references by their literal name are included.
  • It contains a ProcScript instruction in the formatToDbms or formatFromDbms trigger in the Modeled Field.

Static fields and control fields are not included in the field list unless they are included in the component structure.

The default field list for service components includes all fields in the component entity.

Related Links
  • Field List

    Specifies the component entity fields that Uniface manages at runtime. Valid values are Automatic (default), All Fields, and User-defined.

  • Database Connectors

    To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages.

  • Is Static

    Determines whether the field contents are same for all occurrences of the entity.

  • Is Control

    Determines whether the field is a control field in a form component, meaning that it can be selected with the mouse but not get focus. This affects validation behavior.
